title: Coating of tissue Culture Vessels for hPSC

steps: Upon receipt, aliquot Vitronectin and store vials according to manufacturer instructions ( Table 2 ). %s %s Do not store aliquots in frost-free freezer. Do not repeatedly freeze and thaw.
Thaw a vial of Vitronectin at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .

Select extracellular matrix (ECM) according to desired culture conditions. See protocol Maintenance of hPSC for an overview on medium/matrix combinations that have been validated by CorEuStem members. Refer to Table 1 for final volumes needed according to vessel format. %s %s

If not used immediately, coated plates can be stored at 2 °C to 8 °C up to seven days. In our experience, the coating can be stored up to 30 days; however this is not suggested by the manufacturers. For stored plates, prior to plating the cells, allow plates to equilibrate at Room temperature for about 00:20:00 and go to step #9 Do not allow the coated surface to dry out. To prevent excessive evaporation of the coating, tightly seal the plates with Parafilm. Additionally, some DPBS may be added after the incubation time is finished.
Upon receipt, store Matrigel/Geltrex according to the manufacturer indication indicated in the "Materials" section.

Remove an aliquot of Geltrex from -20 °C storage and thaw on wet ice for about 00:30:00 . Optimal concentration depends on cell type as well as the application or research objectives. Check the specific concentration of the batch on the "Certificate of analysis" provided by the manufacturer, and dilute the matrix accordingly. For maintenance of hPSC , a protein concentration of 0.1 mg/mL is a recommended starting concentration. Usually, a dilution of 1:100 is needed for such final concentration.
Mix the content of one vial of 125 µL Matrigel/Geltrex with 12.5 mL of cold DMEM/F-12 in a 15 mL falcon tube. Gently pipette up and down to resuspend the diluted matrix. Keep working on ice.

Dispense diluted Matrigel/Geltrex on the vessel of choice, being careful that the surface is completely covered. If necessary, rock or tap the plate to facilitate this process. Refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes.

Incubate the coated tissue-culture vessels for 00:30:00 at 37 °C or for 01:00:00 at Room temperature . For later use, Matrigel/Geltrex coated plates can be sealed with Parafilm and stored for up to 2 weeks at 4 °C .

If plates were already coated and stored at 4 °C , prior to plating the cells, allow plates to equilibrate at Room temperature for about 00:20:00 and go to step #9 Do not allow the coated surface to dry out. To prevent excessive evaporation of the coating, tightly seal the plates with Parafilm. Additionally, some DMEM/F12 may be added after the incubation time is finished.

After incubation, aspirate the Matrigel/Geltrex solution right before use, by tilting the plate and ensuring not to scrape the bottom of the well. Directly proceed with the plating of cells. Refer to Maintenance of hPSC , and/or to Single cell passaging of hPSC , Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC . Do not allow the coated surface to dry. Matrigel/Geltrex-coated plates cannot be re-used: always seed cells onto fresh Matrigel/Geltrex-coated plates.

In a 15-ml tube, dilute the Vitronectin in 6 mL DPBS, gently pipetting up and down to properly resuspend it. The indicated dilutions are for a final concentration of 5 μg/mL . Vitronectin coating has been tested and validated up to a final concentration of 3.33 μg/mL for hPSC maintenance. Optimal concentration depends on specific hPSC lines, the application or research objectives.
Incubate the coated tissue-culture vessels for 00:30:00 to 01:00:00 at Room temperature .

After incubation, aspirate the Vitronectin solution right before use, by tilting the plate and ensuring not to scrape the bottom of the well. Directly proceed with the plating of cells. Refer to Maintenance of hPSC , and/or to Single cell passaging of hPSC , Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC . Do not allow the coated surface to dry. Vitronectin-coated plates cannot be re-used: always seed cells onto fresh Vitronectin-coated plates.

Dispense diluted Vitronectin on the preferred dish, being careful that the surface is completely covered. If necessary, rock or tap the plate to facilitate this process. Refer to Table 1 for suggested volumes according to vessel format.
Before starting To prevent gelling of Matrigel/Geltrex, pre-chill serological pipettes, tubes and plates to be stored at -20 °C for at least 00:20:00 . Keep working on ice for all the procedures.
Mix Matrigel/Geltrex by slowly inverting the original tube and prepare aliquots according to your needs.
Thaw Matrigel/Geltrex stock on wet ice for 03:00:00 . Alternatively, Matrigel/Geltrex vial can be thawn on wet ice at 4 °C Overnight .
Prepare aliquots according to your needs: the format we suggest in Table 3 for the aliquoting is enough to coat a full 6-well vessel when diluted. %s %s
Follow the appropriate coating procedures depending on the chosen Laminin.
Mix 60 µL of Laminin 521 with 60 mL PBS containing Ca 2+ Mg 2+ in a 15 mL tube (1:100 dilution). Final working concentration = 5 μg/mL .
Incubate the coated cell culture vessel at least 02:00:00 at 37 °C .

Dispense diluted Laminin 521 on the preferred dish, being careful that the surface is completely covered. If necessary, rock or tap the plate to facilitate this process. Refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes according to vessel format.

After incubation, aspirate the Laminin 521 solution right before use, by tilting the plate and ensuring not to scrape the bottom of the well. Directly proceed with the plating of cells. Refer to Maintenance of hPSC , and/or to Single cell passaging of hPSC , Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC . Do not allow the coated surface to dry. Laminin-coated plates cannot be re-used: always seed cells onto fresh Laminin-coated plates.
If not used immediately, coated plates can be stored at 4 °C sealed with Parafilm up to seven days; however, a significant reduction in performance is expected.
Thaw a vial of Laminin 521 at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Mix 57.6 µL of iMatrix-511 with 12 mL PBS without Ca 2+ Mg 2+ in a 50 ml tube. Final working concentration = 2.4 μg/mL .

Dispense diluted iMatrix-511 on the preferred dish, being careful that the surface is completely covered. If necessary, rock or tap the plate to facilitate this process. Refer to Table 1 for recommended volumes according to vessel format.
Incubate the coated cell culture vessel 01:00:00 at 37 °C . In alternative, iMatrix-511 can be incubated at Room temperature for 03:00:00 or at 4 °C Overnight .

After incubation, aspirate the iMatrix-511 solution right before use, by tilting the plate and ensuring not to scrape the bottom of the well. Directly proceed with the plating of cells. Refer to Maintenance of hPSC , and/or to Single cell passaging of hPSC , Non-enzymatic passaging of hPSC . Do not allow the coated surface to dry. Laminin-coated plates cannot be re-used: always seed cells onto fresh Laminin-coated plates.

Prepare aliquots of 125 µL keeping always the tubes on ice. Store at -20 °C . Aliquots of 125µl are sufficient to coat all wells of 2 vessels of 6 wells when diluted. Do not store aliquots in frost-free freezer. Do not repeatedly freeze and thaw.
Upon receipt, store Laminin according to the manufacturer's instructions.
